summary: Task-class → model routing for the inference broker. Local-first (Ollama) for daily / offline; NVIDIA NIM hybrid for frontier coder models that don't fit on local hardware (Patch N).

routine_summary.summary: Cheap log compression + pack rendering. Patch Q (2026-04-27): NIM kimi-k2-instruct primary because it's fast on the free tier (~0.5s smoke vs local 7B's 1-3s for short prompts on M-series Mac) and free for routine work; qwen2.5:7b stays as offline fallback.

patch_builder.summary: Generate code patches in isolated worktrees. Patch N (2026-04-27): NIM enabled, hybrid local+NIM. Local first for offline / daily, NIM frontier coder when it fits the prompt. Order: smaller-fast → bigger-accurate. qwen2.5-coder:14b validated as local primary (selectionRate 2/3 in Patch M comparison batch). qwen2.5:72b is the local diversity alt (1/16, but the wins are real). Patch N.2: dropped qwen/qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct from NIM — endpoint hangs >200s on a 5-token completion (verified 2026-04-27 with direct curl); other NIM models (llama-3.3-70b, kimi-k2, deepseek-v4-flash, qwen3-coder-480b) all respond in <2s.

adversarial_review.summary: Attack a candidate patch. Patch P (2026-04-27): reviewers now distribute round-robin across this list (was: all reviewers used the policy primary). Order matters — reviewer-i uses models[i % len]. kimi-k2-thinking first because it's reasoning-tuned + JSON-disciplined; deepseek-coder local was at 50% validity rate as primary in the prior arrangement and kept producing prose paraphrases instead of structured findings, blocking the noBlockingReview gate on otherwise-clean patches.

research_extraction.summary: Read sources and produce a claim ledger. Patch Q (2026-04-27): NIM kimi-k2-instruct primary — it's instruction-tuned, handles long-context source documents better than qwen2.5:7b, and stays cheap on the free tier. Local qwen2.5:72b is the offline fallback (slow but capable).

merge_arbiter.summary: Final merge recommendation. Stays on the strongest trusted model. Empty until a frontier model is wired explicitly — broker will return a clear error rather than guess.
